item.title: What Trump and Xi Want to Achieve at Their High-Stakes Summit
item.description: The president arrives in Beijing for a two-day meeting with the Chinese leader.

item.title: Trump Faces an Emboldened China in Return to Beijing
item.description: China is increasingly self-sufficient, militarily assertive and economically insulated—and it is digging in on core issues of contention.

item.title: The Factory Town Known as China’s Furniture Capital Is Fighting to Survive
item.description: The U.S. lost much of its furniture industry to China years ago. Now, American tariffs and overseas competition are punishing manufacturers.

item.title: How Xi Is Playing His Iran Cards as Trump Heads to Beijing
item.description: Plus, Beijing wants to be essential to the Middle East without doing anything essential.

item.title: Xi’s China: Dazzling Technology, Military Muscle—and an Economic Mess
item.description: China’s government is pouring money into AI, electric cars and military power, while consumer confidence sags and the job market grows bleak.

item.title: China’s Cars Aren’t in the U.S., but Its Auto Parts Are Everywhere
item.description: Chinese companies have amassed ownership stakes in about 5% of 10,000 auto suppliers in America.

item.title: China’s Export Growth Accelerated in April
item.description: China’s export growth accelerated in April after a brief slowdown in March, reinforcing trade’s role as a key stabilizer of the economy.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
